because.text: Accepted in full; every probe was runnable and reproduced. P1s fixed: actor-desc complement trick was a REAL oracle bug (selected 'a' over 'aa', ValueError on U+0100) - replaced with direct-comparison comparator, collation normative, two pinning vectors; select() now node-bound with node as explicit oracle parameter; future-epoch exclusion made normative (epoch > E not live, before ordering; max_age after); quota semantics pinned to the review's second option (survivors by the SAME policy order + warrant_id asc tiebreak - the unsurprising choice for freshness policies); federated-peer roots REMOVED from v0.6 (future versioned policy tag), prose/schema mismatch gone. P2s: assertion_set_root renamed to set commitment with the rainbow-enumeration caveat (the 'reveals nothing' overclaim retracted, encrypted-blob escape hatch); ViewID declared a coordinate - verifiable projection is (ViewID, set_root) with cache invalidation; APPLY-node override pinned; candidate metadata domains validated by the oracle. Criterion 1 is now executable: FV-BOOK-I-UNREACHABLE replays EV-TV4-IK and requires byte-identity with the Book I suite. Remaining before anchoring at v0.6.0: verification pass over the fixed draft, second-implementation parity with differential harness, Book II s Federation paragraph.

subject.note: Codex Book III impl-gate review: BLOCK verdict - 5 P1 + 4 P2, all confirmed and fixed in-adjudication; suite 21 vectors / 37 checks
